statement: April 2026 cloud rental rates per GPU-hour span roughly $3.99-$8.64 for B200, $4.40 for MI350X (DigitalOcean), and $1.50-$6.98 for MI300X, with H100 referenced at $1.99-$12.29 — a static snapshot of market pricing rather than a stated move.
why: Cloud GPU-hour rates set the marginal cost of renting AI compute and frame the effective price of NVIDIA versus AMD capacity.

impact.mechanism: Rental rates bracket the going market price for AI GPU capacity across vendors.

evidence.source: Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026)
evidence.excerpt: Cloud pricing (per GPU-hr) — B200: $3.99-$8.64; MI350X: $4.40 (DigitalOcean); MI300X: $1.50-$6.98; H100 reference: $1.99-$12.29.

confidence.basis: Secondary source aggregating multiple cloud providers' list rates.

dispersion: Wide per-vendor ranges: B200 $3.99-$8.64, MI300X $1.50-$6.98, H100 $1.99-$12.29; MI350X anchored at a single $4.40 quote.
